joining-multiple-tables.label: Joining Multiple Tables
joining-multiple-tables.summary: Join more than two tables in a single query.

sketch1.title: How to Join More Than Two Tables

sk1.title: Why Join Multiple Tables?
sk1.bodyMarkdown: In real-world databases, information is often split across several tables. To answer complex questions, you may need to combine data from three or more tables in a single query. SQL allows you to chain multiple JOINs together, letting you access all the data you need at once.

sk2.title: How to Join More Than Two Tables
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
To join more than two tables, simply add additional JOIN clauses to your query. Each JOIN connects another table using a matching condition. For example:

```sql
SELECT ...
FROM table1
JOIN table2 ON table1.id = table2.t1_id
JOIN table3 ON table2.id = table3.t2_id;
```
This pattern can be extended to as many tables as needed, as long as you specify the correct relationships.

q1.title: Purpose of Multiple JOINs
q1.prompt: Why would you use more than one JOIN in a SQL query?
q1.hint: Think about what JOINs do in SQL.

help.concept: JOINs are used to combine rows from two or more tables based on related columns.
help.hint_1: Multiple JOINs let you pull data from more than two tables in a single query.
help.hint_2: If you need information that exists in three or more tables, you can use several JOINs to connect them.

options.a: To combine data from several related tables
options.b: To sort the results by multiple columns
options.c: To update multiple tables at once
options.d: To delete rows from several tables

q2.title: Order the Steps: Multi-Table JOIN
q2.prompt: Arrange the steps to join three tables in a SQL query.
q2.hint: Start with the main table, then add JOINs.

help.concept: A multi-table JOIN starts with a base table, then adds JOINs for each additional table.
help.hint_1: After FROM, each JOIN adds another table using ON.
help.hint_2: The SELECT clause can come first or last, but JOINs must follow FROM.

tokens.t1: Write SELECT and FROM with the first table
tokens.t2: Add JOIN for the second table with ON condition
tokens.t3: Add JOIN for the third table with ON condition

q3.title: Fill in the Blank: JOIN Syntax
q3.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to join three tables: 

SELECT * FROM customers ___ orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id ___ payments ON orders.id = payments.order_id;
q3.hint: What keyword is used to add another table to a query?

help.concept: The JOIN keyword is used to add tables to a query and specify how they are related.
help.hint_1: Each new table is connected with JOIN and an ON condition.
help.hint_2: You need the same keyword for both blanks.

q3.template: SELECT * FROM customers ___ orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id ___ payments ON orders.id = payments.order_id;

q4.title: Write a Query: Join Three Tables
q4.prompt: Using the 'products_catalog' dataset, write a SQL query to list each product's name, its category name, and the supplier's name. The relevant tables are products, categories, and suppliers.
q4.hint: You'll need to JOIN products to categories and suppliers using their IDs.

help.concept: To combine columns from three tables, use JOINs with ON conditions that match related columns.
help.hint_1: Start from products, then JOIN categories using category_id, and suppliers using supplier_id.
help.hint_2: Select the name columns from each table after joining them.

q4.starterCode:
-- Write a query to join products, categories, and suppliers
SELECT
